analysis_description: Phase 1 (suggested direction.txt): counterparty-conditioned forward mids on ROUND_4 days 1-3. Script analyze_r4_phase1_counterparty_edges.py joins trades to L1 books, infers aggressor vs bid/ask, forward K in {5,20,100} price rows (same symbol), cross-asset extract fwd at same ts. Outputs r4_phase1_markout_summary.json + sample CSV.
used_to_decide: Phase 1 completion gate object below; no Phase 2 until reviewed.

phase1_bullets.conclusion: Strongest pooled cell: Mark 67 buyer_agg on VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT K=5 mean same-symbol fwd mid +1.96 (n=164, t~13) and K=20 +1.81 (t~5.9); stable per day 1-3 (~1.75-2.2 mean fwd5). Mark 55 seller_agg extract K=100 mean -0.92 (n=595, t~-2.5). Many VEV cells for Mark 22 seller are negative short-horizon (e.g. VEV_5300 K=5 t~-3.4) but smaller n.
Cell-mean baseline leaves mean residual ~0 but large abs mean (~3.19) — heterogeneity within (buyer,seller,symbol,spread-quartile) buckets; not a single additive misspecification.

Mark 01→Mark 22 dominates counts (1339); 2-hop motifs are mostly Mark14↔38 and Mark01→22 interleaved with hydro prints — structural choreography, not a single clean lead-lag edge from this coarse chain alone.
Rows at timestamps with burst_n>=4 show higher mean extract fwd20 (+0.35) vs singleton rows (+0.08) — bursts align with larger short-horizon extract mid drift (overlapping windows caveat).
When buyer is aggressive, Mark 38 shows most negative mean same-symbol fwd20 (-0.14, n=731); Mark 67 most positive (+1.81) — candidate avoid vs lean.

top_5_tradeable_edges.edge: After Mark 67 aggressive BUY on VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT, same-symbol mid rises over next 5-20 price rows (mean ~+1.8 to +2.0; n~164; days 1-3 each positive).
top_5_tradeable_edges.caveat: Mid markout not bid/ask PnL; needs sim with worse fills.

top_5_tradeable_edges.edge: Mark 55 aggressive SELL on extract followed by lower mids at K=100 (mean -0.92, t~-2.5, n=595).
top_5_tradeable_edges.caveat: Long horizon overlaps; confirm with non-overlapping event study.

top_5_tradeable_edges.edge: Mark 22 aggressive SELL on VEV_5300: negative short-horizon same-symbol fwd (K=5 mean -0.20, t~-3.4, n=161).
top_5_tradeable_edges.caveat: Small n vs extract; may be inventory pressure on wing.

top_5_tradeable_edges.edge: Multi-symbol bursts (>=4 prints same ts) associate with higher extract fwd20 vs singletons (+0.35 vs +0.08).
top_5_tradeable_edges.caveat: Overlapping forward windows; use event clustering next.

top_5_tradeable_edges.edge: Mark 38 buyer_agg prints lean to negative same-symbol fwd20 (-0.14) while Mark 67 buyer_agg strongly positive — liquidity-taking asymmetry.
top_5_tradeable_edges.caveat: Coarse aggressor inference; refine with trade-through.

negative_or_null_findings: Mark 01→Mark 22 on VEV_5300 in burst_ge4 subsample: mean fwd20 slightly negative (-0.12, n=131) — basket ladder not a simple positive drift on that slice.
M01→M22 VEV_5300 singleton bucket n=0 (all 5300 prints in sample are burst-associated) — cannot compare burst vs singleton for that exact pair.
Residual mean near zero after rich (buyer,seller,sym,spr_q) cell means — no single global additive anomaly; edges are local/cell-specific.

analysis_description: Phase 2 scripts: (1) Mark01→Mark22 burst≥4 vs singleton control — forward VEV_5300 mids; (2) hourly signed flow vs next-hour extract return correlation; (3) IV gap 5200 vs 5300 vs M01→22 trade density by hour; (4) VEV_5300 spread buckets vs mean |fwd20 mid|. Pilot trader_v1 loads trade CSVs by timestamp (union across days for overlapping ts) because backtester leaves market_trades empty.
used_to_decide: Phase 2 completion gate below; sim pilot falsifies naive copy of Phase-1 mid edge at touch without inventory.

phase1_interaction: Phase-1 Mark67 buyer_agg extract mid markouts stay positive in tape; Phase-2 pilot trader_v1 (buy on Mark67 aggressive tape + sell on Mark55 aggressive tape) loses -2908 worse / -2960 all — mid predictability does not survive worse-fill execution without richer state. Mark01→22 burst slice now shows **positive** VEV_5300 fwd vs singleton control (+0.33 vs -0.001 at K=20), refining Phase-1's negative burst-only note for 5300 (that was M01→22 on 5300 only; here we use any M01→22 in burst).

phase2_bullets.conclusion: 292 timestamps with ≥4 prints including Mark01→Mark22 row: mean VEV_5300 fwd20 +0.33 vs singleton control +0.00 — basket bursts align with short-horizon 5300 lift.
Tight L1 spread (≤2) on 5300 has **lower** mean |fwd20| mid change (1.25) than 3–6 tick bucket (1.45) — tight book is calmer, not higher vol, on this tape slice.
Corr(5300 signed flow hour h, extract hour return h+1) ≈ +0.05 on n=27 hour-pairs — weak; not a standalone edge.
Hourly |IV5200−IV5300| vs M01→22 trade counts — tables in JSON for regime overlays (steepness co-occurs with basket activity in some bins).
Counterparty-triggered touch trades on extract are **negative** PnL under worse fills — do not ship naive Phase-1 copy rule.

top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2.edge: After Mark01→Mark22 multi-print burst (≥4 rows at ts), VEV_5300 mid drifts up vs singleton timestamps (fwd20 ~+0.33 vs ~0).
top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2.orthogonal_to_phase1: Uses burst orchestration, not only Mark67 extract cell.
top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2.caveat: Control is random singleton sample, not matched propensity.

top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2.edge: Tight 5300 spread regime is lower realized |Δmid| over 20 rows — useful as **vol-off** filter for other signals.
Mark55 seller_agg on extract still shows negative long-horizon mid markout in Phase-1 table — Phase-2 sim suggests fading at touch needs bid improvement logic.
top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2.orthogonal_to_phase1: Confirms adverse-selection story; sim loss on naive sell trigger.

top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2.edge: Hourly 5300 signed flow vs next-hour extract return correlation near zero — deprioritize simple Granger-style hourly pipeline.
IV gap hour table (r4_phase2_iv_mark_density.json) — combine with burst gate in Phase 3 Sonic conditioning.

analysis_description: Phase 3: Sonic joint gate on Round 4 (same definition as R3 script: ask1-bid1 on L1 inner-join 5200+5300+extract per timestamp). analyze_r4_phase3_sonic_gate_r4.py recomputes extract fwd20 tight vs loose, Mark67×gate, M01→22 on 5300×gate, burst×gate×extract; inclineGod spread–spread Pearson full/tight/loose subsamples.
used_to_decide: Phase 3 completion gate below; sim trader_v2 tests gate+Mark67 vs ungated v1.

comparison_to_phase1_ungated: Ungated Phase-1 pooled Mark67 buyer_agg extract fwd20 mean ~+1.81 (K=20, n=164). With joint gate ON at trade timestamp: mean fwd20 **+2.06** but only **n=27** (r4_phase3_gate_summary.json); loose bucket mean **+1.77** (n=137). Gate **amplifies** the mid markout on this subsample but **shrinks n** — consistent with Sonic (cleaner, fewer events).
comparison_to_phase2_ungated: Phase-2 burst extract fwd20 (ungated) mean +0.35 vs singleton +0.08. Under gate **tight** at burst ts: mean **+0.33** (n=292); loose burst row n=1 only (unstable). Gate does **not** replace burst logic; it **stacks** on extract forward (population mean tight +0.32 vs loose -0.12, Welch t~7.94).
inclineGod_spread_spread: Pearson s5200–s5300: **0.30** all sample, **0.19** tight-only, **0.05** loose-only (subsample every 5th ts). Voucher spreads co-move less when both already tight; extract spread vs 5200/5300 ~0 (weak linear link).
three_way_thesis: Primary Round-4 candidate: **(joint tight gate, extract forward drift)** is large and significant on tape; **(Mark67 aggressive buy, gate on)** shows **higher** mean fwd20 than gate-off (+2.06 vs +1.77) but small n. **(M01→M22, VEV_5300, gate on)** mean fwd20 slightly negative (-0.12, n=132); **all** M01→M22 5300 prints in sample occur under tight gate (n_loose=0) — basket runs in tight surface; not a simple long 5300 after print.
tier_a_sim: trader_v2: gate on → buy on Mark67 tape signal; gate off → flatten extract. Round4 total PnL **-1244** worse and all (vs v1 **-2908** worse) — gate **reduces** churn/loss vs ungated counterparty copy but still **not** profitable at touch; Tier-A evidence is **tape** not **PnL** yet.

ranked_edges_phase3.edge: Extract K=20 mid forward: mean **+0.32** when Sonic gate tight vs **-0.12** when loose (P_tight~0.26, Welch t~7.9, days 1-3).
ranked_edges_phase3.gate_cleans_signal: yes for population extract fwd — large positive gap vs Phase1/2 unconditioned loose mean.

ranked_edges_phase3.edge: Mark67 buyer_agg extract fwd20 mean **+2.06** when gate tight vs **+1.77** loose (small tight n).
ranked_edges_phase3.gate_cleans_signal: partial — directionally aligns with Sonic; sample thin when tight.

ranked_edges_phase3.edge: Spread–spread: s5200–s5300 correlation **drops** in tight subsample (co-movement muted when both books already narrow).
ranked_edges_phase3.gate_cleans_signal: inclineGod-style object: book geometry changes by regime.

ranked_edges_phase3.edge: M01→M22 on VEV_5300 under tight gate: mean fwd20 **-0.12** (n=132) — fade-long-5300-after-basket not supported by mid alone.
ranked_edges_phase3.gate_cleans_signal: clarifies structure: basket prints live in tight regime but short-horizon 5300 drift not strongly positive here.

ranked_edges_phase3.edge: Sim: gated Mark67 pilot (trader_v2) loses less than ungated v1 (-1244 vs -2908 worse) but still negative — execution layer still missing.
ranked_edges_phase3.gate_cleans_signal: risk filter helps vs naive Phase-2 bot; does not yet monetize mid edge.

analysis_description: Post-Phase-3 extension: (1) per-day Sonic gate extract fwd20 tight vs loose means (r4_phase3_gate_extract_by_day.json) — all three days show positive tight-minus-loose gap (~0.48-0.65 ticks). (2) trader_v3: three-way pilot — joint gate AND Mark01→Mark22 burst (>=4 rows at ts) → long VEV_5300 at ask; flatten when gate or burst off. Trades indexed by (csv_day,timestamp) from separate day files to avoid cross-day timestamp collision in union index.
used_to_decide: Day-stability confirms gate+extract mid story on every tape day; sim v3 still loses on 5300 touch (-1131 worse) — burst mid edge does not survive worse fills with naive buy-at-ask; next would be passive join or smaller size / limit logic.
